query: Walmart job posting (artificial intelligence OR machine learning OR generative AI OR LLM OR data scientist)

results.title: Principal, Data Scientist
highlights:
, Data Scientist
[...]
About the RoleWe are looking for a Principal Data Scientist to lead high-impact initiatives within Walmart’s Decision Science organization. This role is centered on building scalable, end-to-end Data Science and AI solutions that improve customer experience, optimize operational and sourcing decisions, and drive measurable business impact across the enterprise.As a senior technical leader, you will partner closely with business, product, engineering, and operations teams throughout the entire lifecycle of a solution — from problem formulation and opportunity identification to model development, experimentation, deployment, and impact measurement. You will help define success metrics, design rigorous experiments, develop scalable AI/ML systems, and influence strategic decision-making through data-driven insights and innovation.Our team is deeply customer-focused and works on some of the most critical challenges in retail and supply chain, building intelligent systems that improve item availability, enable smarter sourcing and inventory decisions, optimize operational efficiency, and ultimately enhance the end-to-end customer experience. We are investing heavily in GenAI-powered capabilities that improve business insights, accelerate decision-making, and enhance stakeholder productivity through intelligent analytics, conversational experiences, and AI-assisted workflows.You will play a key role in shaping the long-term data science and AI vision of Walmart, driving innovation across both traditional ML and GenAI applications, and raising the technical bar across the organization.
[...]
and AI solutions.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy end-to-end ML and data science products, including problem framing, feature engineering, model development, experimentation, deployment, and measurement on business metrics.
[...]
- Develop and deploy production-grade GenAI solutions using modern LLMs and agent orchestration frameworks, including prompt engineering, tool integration, skills and retrieval-based workflows, to improve business insights, automate operational workflows, and enhance decision-making.
[...]
- 10+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, Advanced Analytics, or related disciplines, with a proven track record of leading large-scale, high-impact initiatives.
[...]
- Strong expertise in statistical modeling, machine learning, experimentation, causal inference, and data science methodologies.
[...]
- Experience building and deploying scalable machine learning solutions and AI-powered applications in production environments.
[...]
- Hands-on experience developing GenAI solutions using Large Language Models (LLMs), prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), Skills, vector databases, and agentic workflows.
[...]
- Experience with cloud AI/ML platforms and modern AI tooling ecosystems, preferably on GCP or Azure.
[...]
- Experience with MLOps and LLMOps practices, including feature stores, model monitoring, evaluation frameworks, and production ML systems.
[...]
Data science, machine learning, optimization models, PhD in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Information Technology, Operations Research, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Econometrics, Publications or active peer reviewer in related journals or conference, Successful completion of one or more assessments in Python, Spark, Scala, or R, Using open source frameworks (for example, scikit learn, tensorflow, torch), We value candidates with a background in creating inclusive digital experiences, demonstrating knowledge in implementing Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 AA standards, assistive technologies, and integrating digital accessibility seamlessly. The ideal candidate would have knowledge of accessibility best practices and join us as we continue to create accessible products and services following Walmart’s accessibility standards and guidelines for supporting an inclusive culture.

# Staff, Data Engineer - Customer Insights & GenAI
[...]
As a Staff Data Engineer, you will lead data-driven projects and platform initiatives while taking ownership of end-to-end technical delivery. You will serve as the technical expert for your team and work closely with product stakeholders and senior leadership to align on strategic priorities and deliver measurable business outcomes. This role will play a critical part in transforming customer survey and feedback data into actionable business intelligence by building scalable data and AI platform capabilities that power intelligent automation, GenAI-powered experiences, and Agentic AI workflows across customer and business operations.You will lead the development of next-generation Voice of Customer (VoC) solutions, including AI-powered VoC Agents that leverage structured and unstructured customer feedback to generate proactive insights, identify emerging trends, and enable data-driven decision-making across the enterprise. Operating at the intersection of large-scale distributed data platforms, near real-time intelligence, and AI-powered decision systems, you will help deliver advanced automation, intelligent insights, and enhanced customer experiences that drive impact across Walmart’s ecosystem.About the Team:By bringing the objective voice of the Walmart customer to all aspects of the business, the Customer Insights team is the trusted source of integrated customer insights for Walmart. The MyCustomer team brings the voice of the Walmart customer into every aspect of the business by delivering actionable customer insights and experience intelligence at enterprise scale. Our team builds scalable data and AI-driven platforms that transform customer feedback into meaningful insights, enabling teams across Walmart to make faster, customer-centric decisions.We are focused on modernizing the VoC ecosystem through advanced analytics, GenAI-powered experiences, and Agentic AI capabilities. As part of this team, you will help develop next-generation AI-powered VoC solutions that convert structured and unstructured customer feedback into proactive insights, trend detection, and intelligent recommendations that drive business impact across Walmart.
[...]
- Design and develop AI-powered Voice of Customer (VoC) solutions, including GenAI and Agentic AI workflows that enable proactive insights, intelligent recommendations, and automated decision support.
[...]
- Build and operationalize intelligent agents leveraging LLMs, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), semantic search, and enterprise knowledge systems to enhance customer and business intelligence capabilities.
[...]
- Employ modern ML, LLM, GenAI, and Agentic AI frameworks, tools, and platforms to accelerate intelligent automation and customer insight generation.
[...]
- Strong expertise in Machine Learning, Data Science, Generative AI, LLMs, and emerging Agentic AI frameworks and workflows.
[...]
Option 1: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science and 4 years' experience in software engineering or related field. Option 2: 6 years’ experience in software engineering or related field. results.title: Staff, Data Scientist
highlights:
# Staff, Data Scientist
[...]
About the Role We’re looking for a Staff Data Scientist to design and build advanced retrieval, relevance, ranking, bidding, targeting recommendation models to ensure good Walmart customer and advertiser’s experience that power critical advertising business growth. You’ll build and deploy state-of-the-art prediction models (classical + ML + deep learning), drive explainability and trust (XAI), and explore next-generation approaches such as foundation models for unifying all different tasks. You’ll partner closely with engineering, product, and stakeholders to deliver measurable impact at scale.
[...]
Develop advanced time series solutions using:
[...]
- Statistical methods (ETS, ARIMA/SARIMA, State Space Models)
- ML approaches (GBMs, Random Forests, linear/elastic models with engineered time features)
- Deep learning and foundation model (FM, MMOE, PLE, HSTU)
- Probabilistic forecasting and uncertainty quantification (quantile regression, Bayesian approaches, conformal prediction, prediction intervals)
[...]
- Mentor other data scientists, set modeling standards, and influence technical direction across teams.
- 8+ years in data science / applied ML (or PhD + 5 years), with deep hands-on exposure to forecasting and predictive modeling.
- Demonstrated experience delivering production grade ML models with measurable business outcomes.
- Hands-on experience with deep learning frameworks (PyTorch or TensorFlow) and modern architectures for time series.
[...]
- Practical experience with explainable AI methods and communicating model reasoning to non-technical stakeholders.
[...]
- Experience with graph neural networks (PyG/DGL), spatiotemporal GNNs, or temporal graph learning.
- Experience with causal inference or decision-focused forecasting (uplift, impact estimation, counterfactuals, policy evaluation).
- Familiarity with large-scale data/compute: Spark, distributed training, feature stores, GPU workflows.
- Experience building human-centered explainability: dashboards, driver decomposition, “why changed” analysis, model cards.
[...]
- Publications, patents, or open-source contributions in time series, XAI, or graph learning.
[...]
- Experience with data platforms like Spark/Databricks, Airflow, Kubernetes
- MLOps/AgentOps experience in deployment model and/or Agentic workflows at scale.
[...]
may be subject to a specific plan or program terms. For information about benefits and eligibility, see One.Walmart. The annual salary range for this position is $143,000.00 - $286,000.00 Additional compensation includes annual or quarterly performance bonuses. Additional compensation for certain positions
[...]
Option 1: Bachelors degree in Statistics, Economics, Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology or related field and 4 years' experience in an analytics related field. Option 2: Masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology or related field and 2 years' experience in an analytics related field. Option 3: 6 years' experience in an analytics or related field
[...]

results.title: Senior Director, Data Science & Ontology
highlights:
At Walmart, data and AI shape decisions that improve experiences for millions of customers, members, and associates. The Senior Director, Data Science & Ontology will define and scale Walmart’s semantic intelligence strategy, bringing together data science, ontology, taxonomy, knowledge graphs, machine learning, and responsible AI to make Walmart’s data more connected, discoverable, trusted, and actionable. This leader will set the multi-year vision for ontology-driven data products and AI capabilities, lead high-performing teams, and partner with product, engineering, merchandising, search, catalog, operations, governance, and executive stakeholders to deliver measurable business impact at enterprise scale.
[...]
Lead Enterprise Data Science and Ontology StrategyDefine and execute a multi-year strategy for ontology, taxonomy, knowledge graph, semantic search, metadata, and AI-enabled data products. Translate Walmart’s business priorities into scalable semantic data capabilities that improve customer experiences, associate productivity, operational efficiency, and decision quality.Build Scalable Semantic and AI SystemsLead the development of ontology-driven systems that connect structured and unstructured data across complex business domains. Guide teams building capabilities such as entity resolution, classification, product and content enrichment, semantic search, recommendation relevance, knowledge graphs, embeddings, LLM-enabled workflows, RAG, and intelligent data discovery.Own Data Quality, Governance, and Responsible AIEstablish standards for ontology design, taxonomy governance, metadata quality, lineage, stewardship, measurement, and model governance. Ensure data science and AI systems are explainable, reliable, monitored, auditable, and aligned with Walmart’s standards for integrity, privacy, compliance, and responsible technology use.Drive Business Impact and Executive AlignmentPartner with senior leaders across product, engineering, merchandising, operations, customer experience, and business teams to identify high-value opportunities, define success metrics, build business cases, and deliver measurable ROI. Communicate complex technical concepts clearly to executive, technical, and non-technical audiences.Lead the End-to-End Data Science LifecycleOversee problem formulation, exploratory analysis, feature strategy, ontology design, model development, experimentation, validation, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement. Ensure teams use appropriate statistical, machine learning, optimization, and AI techniques to solve high-scale business problems.Scale Teams, Talent, and Operating ModelsBuild, lead, and develop a world-class team of data scientists, ontology specialists, applied scientists, machine learning practitioners, data strategists, and technical leaders. Create a high-performance culture through hiring, coaching, mentoring, succession planning, capability building, and clear accountability.Champion Innovation at Walmart ScaleStay current with emerging practices in data science, ontology engineering, generative AI, knowledge graphs, MLOps, semantic data platforms, and responsible AI. Identify where new technologies can create competitive advantage, while ensuring practical adoption, measurable impact, and operational excellence.What You’ll Bring…
[...]
- 12+ years of experience in data science, machine learning, applied AI, ontology, knowledge graphs, search, recommendations, data platforms, or related technical domains.
[...]
- Strong applied AI and machine learning foundation, including experience with NLP, embeddings, classification, ranking, recommendation systems, experimentation, model evaluation, and production model monitoring.
[...]
- Experience applying generative AI, LLMs, RAG, vector search, agentic workflows, or embeddings to enterprise data, product data, or knowledge systems.

results.title: Staff, Data Scientist
highlights:
Staff, Data Scientist
[...]
# Staff, Data Scientist
[...]
About Decision Sciences The Decision Sciences team provides comprehensive analytical solutions, empowering our business customers to make data-driven decisions in areas such as Supply Chain, Store Operation, Last Mile Delivery, and Innovation & Transformation. We offer measurable value to all stakeholders by delivering accurate and actionable insights that stimulate growth, enhance efficiency, and guide strategic planning. We are dedicated to staying at the cutting edge of data & technology, constantly offering innovative solutions that enhance the experience for both associates and customers. We are looking for a Staff Data Scientist to join Decision Sciences team. As a Staff Data Scientist, you will be responsible for modeling complex business problems and discovering insights through machine learning, statistical modeling, simulation, and optimization that empower our customers across Walmart to make data-driven decisions. You will drive data science partnership across US, Costa Rica, and India locations. You will be tasked to set goals, create a strategy, and closely collaborate with Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Governance teams and business stakeholders to deliver best-in-class analytical tools and solutions. What You’ll Do
[...]
- Model Development: Applying sophisticated data science and advanced analytics models such as machine learning, statistical modeling, simulation, and optimization to produce valuable insights that guide decision-making and enhance business outcomes.
[...]
- Machine Learning Operations (MLOps): Deploying and maintaining machine learning models in production environments, ensuring reliability and scalability.
[...]
- 5+ years of experience in utilizing advanced analytics to solve business problems, with a focus on understanding causal relationships
[...]
- Demonstrated expertise in developing advanced data science models and leading data-driven projects from conception to implementation, driving and influencing project roadmaps.
[...]
- Strong ability to translate business problems into causal frameworks and estimate impact
[...]
- Proficient coding in one or more languages: Java, C++, Python, Spark, Scala, or R
[...]
- Strong proficiency in SQL for data manipulation and querying.
[...]
- Proficient with data warehouse like BigQuery and cloud platforms (GCP, Azure).
[...]
- Experience in MLOps and deploying/managing machine learning models.
[...]
- Experience mentoring junior operations research or machine learning scientists.
[...]
Option 1: Bachelors degree in Statistics, Economics, Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology or related field and 4 years' experience in an analytics related field. Option 2: Masters degree in Statistics, Economics, Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Technology or related field and 2 years' experience in an analytics related field. Option 3: 6 years' experience in an analytics or related field
[...]
Data science, machine learning, optimization models, PhD in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Information Technology, Operations Research, Statistics, Applied Mathematics, Econometrics, Successful completion of one or more assessments in Python, Spark, Scala, or R, Using open source frameworks (for example, scikit learn, tensorflow, torch), We value candidates with a background in creating inclusive digital experiences, demonstrating knowledge in implementing Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 AA standards, assistive technologies, and integrating digital accessibility seamlessly. The ideal candidate would have knowledge of accessibility best practices and join us as we continue to create accessible products and services following Walmart’s accessibility standards and guidelines for supporting an inclusive culture.

# (USA) Principal, Data Scientist
[...]
Job Description SummaryAs a Principal Data Scientist and Core Solution Architect, you will be the visionary lead responsible for defining the technical backbone of Walmart’s Retail Digital Twin ecosystem. You will move beyond being a "user" of tools to becoming an architect of systems—selecting and integrating best-in-class simulation engines, 3D frameworks, and agentic AI to build a high-fidelity, scalable platform that optimizes the world’s largest retail operation.
[...]
- Architect End-to-End Systems: Lead the design of a modular, distributed digital twin architecture that balances simulation fidelity with computational efficiency across diverse retail use cases.
[...]
- Select & Optimize the Tech Stack: Evaluate and deploy the most effective simulation paradigms (Agent-Based, DES, Physics-based) and tools—whether leveraging AnyLogic, NVIDIA Omniverse, or building custom C++/Python-based engines from the ground up.
[...]
- Drive Software Engineering Excellence: Build production-grade, scalable applications that integrate real-time IoT data streams, ensuring the digital twin platform is performant and maintainable at an enterprise scale.
[...]
- Lead Agentic AI Strategy: Design the orchestration layer for agentic workflows, enabling intelligent entities within the twin to perform complex reasoning and autonomous optimization.
[...]
- Cross-Functional Technical Leadership: Serve as the primary technical liaison between software engineering, data science, and retail operations to align the simulation roadmap with global business objectives.
[...]
- System Architecture Mastery: 8+ years of experience designing complex, large-scale software systems or simulation environments with a focus on modularity and high-performance computing.

[...]
- Agentic AI & Optimization: Proven track record of implementing Reinforcement Learning (RL), Multi-Agent Systems (MAS), or Large Action Models (LAMs) within a simulation context.
[...]
- Academic Pedigree: PhD or MS in Computer Science, Robotics, Computational Physics, or Industrial Engineering.

highlights:

# (USA) Senior, Software Engineer - Agent Builder
[...]
About the Team Walmart is transforming how we solve business problems and introducing our first “biz/tech” role. We are enabling a future where a single individual can own, design, and deploy powerful AI-driven agents—without requiring a full team or deep technical background. We are looking for Agent Builders—problem solvers who are passionate about speed, simplification, and impact. As an Agent Builder, you will leverage our self-service agent-building tools—featuring low-code/no-code and natural language interfaces—to design and deploy agents that automate complex workflows across Walmart. You will solve large-scale problems end-to-end, iterate rapidly, and significantly improve operational efficiency. This role is ideal for self-starters who think holistically, simplify fearlessly, and execute with urgency. What You’ll Do
[...]
- Use a low-code/no-code platform to build and deploy software agents that solve complex business problems
[...]
- Translate business challenges into agent-driven automation by orchestrating APIs, triggers, LLM functions, and data workflows
[...]
- Rapidly iterate on agent designs using natural language interfaces and workflow visualizers
[...]
- Own solutions from start to finish—no handoffs, no dependencies—focused on delivering results
[...]
- Partner with cross-functional teams to understand upstream and downstream impacts of automation
[...]
- Continuously improve agent performance by monitoring execution and applying first-principles thinking to optimize or re-architect solutions
[...]
- Champion change by challenging legacy thinking, pushing boundaries, and driving action
[...]
- Act as a force multiplier by enabling business stakeholders to adopt agent-based thinking and automation
[...]
- Your success will be measured by sales growth, cost savings, and time savings, not the
[...]
of agents you build
[...]
- 5+ years of experience in business, operations, product, strategy, or a generalist role, with a proven track record of delivering results
- Familiarity with core technical concepts (e.g., APIs, microservices, LLMs, triggers, webhooks)
- Strong analytical skills with a systems-thinking mindset
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills
- Ability to think independently and simplify complex problems using first principles
[...]
- Experience with AI/LLM applications, prompt engineering, or automation tools
- Understanding of retail, eCommerce, supply chain, merchandising, or logistics operations
- Experience in a fast-paced, high-growth environment
[...]
Option 1: Bachelor's degree in computer science, computer engineering, computer information systems, software engineering, or related area and 3 years’ experience in software engineering or related area.Option 2: 5 years’ experience in software engineering or related area.
[...]
Master’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Computer Information Systems, Software Engineering, or related area and 1 year's experience in software engineering or related area., We value candidates with a background in creating inclusive digital experiences, demonstrating knowledge in implementing Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 AA standards, assistive technologies, and integrating digital accessibility seamlessly. results.title: Staff, Software Engineer
highlights:
# Staff, Software Engineer
[...]
As a Staff Software Engineer for Walmart’s Developer AI tool, Wibey, you will partner with other technical leaders to design, build, and iterate on the Wibey Core AI orchestration layers that enable intelligent, context-aware, and personalized developer experiences across Walmart’s global ecosystem. You'll bring deep technical execution to the intersection of AI systems, memory/context management, and multi-agent orchestration – turning architectural vision into working, production-quality software that shapes the future of developer productivity at scale.About the team:The Wibey Core team is a high-impact group of engineers who design and operate the intelligence layer powering Wibey, Walmart's AI super agent for builders. They sit at the intersection of cutting-edge AI and large-scale platform engineering, solving genuinely hard problems that make a measurable difference for our builders every day.What you'll do:
[...]
- Implement and iterate on complex problems in AI orchestration and context management, building the systems that connect multiple AI agents, MCP servers, and context pipelines while maintaining stateful, secure, and relevant contextual understanding across Wibey interfaces.
[...]
- Collaborate with technical leaders and product teams to contribute to technical roadmaps, author and review ADRs (Architectural Decision Records), build POCs and implement them in production.
[...]
- Partner with cross-functional engineering and product teams to translate requirements and architectural direction into well-designed, maintainable, and scalable solutions.
[...]
- Mentor junior and mid-level engineers on the team, helping grow technical craft and accelerating their contributions to Wibey Core.
[...]
What you'll bring:
[...]
- Experienced full stack development in NodeJS, Python, React, Java, Spring or similar
- Deep expertise in AI systems architecture, including multi-agent orchestration, language model interactions, context persistence, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) – with a track record of shipping these capabilities in production environments.
[...]
- Solid understanding of context and memory management in AI applications, including vector databases, embeddings, and session-state design.
[...]
- Ability to engage meaningfully in architectural discussions, influence technical direction, and contribute to design decisions alongside senior technical leaders.
[...]
- Exceptional communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across engineering and product teams and clearly articulate technical tradeoffs.
[...]

results.title: (USA) Staff, Software Engineer
highlights:
(USA) Staff, Software Engineer
[...]
# (USA) Staff, Software Engineer
[...]
Role summary:We are transforming how we solve challenging business and technical problems in Growth Tech by maximizing agentic development tools. We will use AI coding agents extensively across plan, spec, design, implementation, testing, refactoring, documentation, and operational workflows while developing practical standards for building scalable, reliable, secure production systems. We are looking for an experienced software engineer who can ship high-quality systems with Agentic coding tools. As a Staff Software Engineer, you will design, prototype, build, deploy, and improve next-generation intelligent systems that blend software, models, and automation into cohesive, adaptive platforms.
[...]
About the team:Our team operates at the intersection of software engineering, data, and AI systems, focusing on rapid experimentation and platform development for intelligent automation at scale. We deliver adaptive, production-ready software that evolves through usage, emphasizing iteration speed and measurable outcomes. Tackling complex technical challenges, the team designs scalable, reliable, and secure systems. results.title: (USA) Staff, Product Manager
highlights:
Walmart International Product is a transformational team at the forefront of retail innovation, focused on redefining how enterprise sourcing, compliance, and product development leverage data and AI drive smarter decisions. We are seeking a dynamic Staff Product Manager with a strong background in AI/ML, data platforms, and enterprise products to own and scale a key component within our Sourcing Intelligence platform. This role offers the opportunity to work across business and technical domains, shaping solutions that unlock real-time insights, improve agility, and create lasting impact across a global retail organization.We are looking for a strategic, innovative, AI-savvy Staff Product Manager to own and deliver a key product or component within our transformative products Sourcing Intelligence platform. This role sits at the intersection of AI, data, and supply chain operations driving solutions that turn fragmented information into enterprise intelligence to accelerate decision-making, reduce risk, and unlock new business value.You will be responsible for defining and executing a product strategy that empowers the organization to move from reactive to proactive sourcing through AI-driven insights, semantic data access, and self-service analytics. You’ll partner cross-functionally with engineering, data science, design, and business stakeholders to shape the vision, validate problems, and deliver impactful solutions on a scale.This is a high-impact, high-visibility role where you’ll help reimagine how the enterprise leverages data and intelligence to operate faster, smarter, and more resiliently.
[...]
- Own a specific product, service, or capability within the broader Sourcing Intelligence platform.
[...]
- Contribute to the overall platform strategy by identifying use cases where AI, data, and automation can deliver meaningful impact.
[...]
- Use AI tools and rapid prototyping techniques to validate ideas and accelerate feedback loops with stakeholders.
[...]
- Contribute to AI enablement across the platform, including intelligent agents, contextual analytics, and semantic layer utilization.
[...]
- 5+ years of product management experience, including 2+ years building AI/ML, data, or analytics-driven products.
- Proven experience applying AI particularly LLMs, intelligent agents, and data products to solve complex business problems.
[...]
- Proven ability to define, build, and scale enterprise products in cross-functional environments.
[...]
- Strong understanding of data platforms, including semantic layers, data pipelines, and analytics tools.
[...]
- Familiarity with semantic layers, vector databases, or embedded LLMs.
- Experience in retail, supply chain, or sourcing environments.
- Technical fluency and the ability to engage confidently in architectural and system design discussions.
- Experience leading or contributing to platforms or internal tools used across multiple teams or domains.

Option 1: Bachelor’s degree in computer science, engineering, or related area and 7 years’ experience in product management. Option 2: 9 years’ experience in product management or related area.

highlights:
# Distinguished, Software Engineer, Growth Tech
[...]
Position Summary We are transforming how we solve challenging and impactful business and technical problems in Growth Tech by maximizing agentic development tools such as Code Puppy, Claude Code and OpenAI Codex. We will use AI coding agents extensively across plan, spec, design, implementation, testing, refactoring, documentation, and operational workflows while developing practical standards for building scalable, reliable, secure production systems.We are looking for an experienced software engineer and technical leader who can both ship high-quality, scalable systems and define the engineering practices for AI-agent-native development. As the Distinguished Software Engineer, you will lead the team in redesigning how work is specified, delegated to coding agents, reviewed by humans, tested, integrated, secured, and released, while embedding system architecture best practices directly into agentic coding workflows. You will enhance the team's effectiveness through mentorship, collaboration, and thought leadership, advocating for ideas that optimize for long-term success while maintaining business momentum. Your role will have a significant impact on the organization and Walmart's business growth. If you thrive in a dynamic environment and are eager to lead and innovate, we invite you to apply.About the team Our team operates at the intersection of software engineering, data, AI systems, and rapid experimentation. We build platforms that enable intelligent automation at scale, ranging from agent orchestration to customer-facing AI systems.We emphasize iteration speed, practical impact, and systems that learn from usage. The work often involves ambiguous problem spaces where we define patterns as we build them.

# Director, Data Science, Network Design - International Supply Chain
[...]
Lead the data science and optimization engine behind International Supply Chain network design. You’ll turn complex questions on fulfillment nodes, inventory placement, and capacity strategy into scalable analytical solutions that improve delivery speed, cost‑to‑serve, and network performance. This is a high‑impact role partnering with Network Design and senior Supply Chain leaders to shape investment decisions and long‑term strategy.
[...]
International Supply Chain Network Design builds the blueprint for how our fulfillment network evolves to meet customer demand efficiently and reliably. The team combines strategy, analytics, and cross‑functional partnership to evaluate scenarios, quantify tradeoffs, and guide decisions across nodes, lanes, and capacity. This role elevates decision quality by bringing rigorous modeling, strong data foundations, and executive‑ready insights to the table.

- Build, validate, and deploy models that quantify tradeoffs, ROI, and performance impacts.

- Lead and mentor data science talent; establish analytics and modeling best practices.

you’ll bring

# Principal, Data Scientist
[...]
PrincipalSeniorStaffML / AIData Science
[...]
Principal-level role leading AI/ML and operations research initiatives across Walmart US, with focus on designing large-scale causal inference solutions, agentic AI systems, and driving measurable business impact across associate workforce planning and operational decision-making.
[...]
The Principal Data Scientist will lead AI and machine learning (ML) initiatives to enhance operational decision-making for Walmart US. Key responsibilities include designing and prototyping advanced ML/AI solutions, collaborating with cross-functional teams to scale and implement these models, and ensuring (consulting) ongoing model monitoring and management. The role requires close partnership with various Walmart teams (such as engineering, product, Store Ops, tech data science teams) and focuses on aligning AI solutions with strategic business goals, improving associate efficiency, and driving measurable business impact. The Principal Data Scientist is also responsible for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of data science processes, including model development, accuracy, reliability, and cost savings.## What you'll do...
[...]
· Apply broad knowledge of ML/AI, Operation Research, GenAI and agentic AI to identify growth areas, new and emerging opportunities. Conducts research by studying organization goals, strategies, practices, and user projects. Recommends strategies by evaluating organization outcomes; identifying problems; evaluating trends; anticipating requirements. 
· Influence a team of Data Scientists focused on powering AI/ML solutions within the scope of associate work prioritization, workforce planning and retention strategies.
[...]
· Partner closely with other Data Science, product, and engineering teams to inform, drive and accelerate innovations in discovery experiences via Insights, frameworks, causal inference solutions and machine learning prototypes.
[...]
· Experience in the development of large-scale ML/AI solutions, Causal Inference. 
· 6 years+ of experience in Applied ML familiar with core supervised and unsupervised algorithms. 
· Skilled in Programming language (Python) and Large-scale training using data structures and algorithms. 
· 2 years+ experience with Causal Inference frameworks, Bayesian Networks and Operations Research methods (Newsvendor problem, Travelling Salesman problem, Shortest Path models, etc.) · Agentic AI experience is a plus.

results.title: (USA) Principal, Data Scientist | Hireza
highlights: commerce: empowering customers to discover high-quality, price-competitive products that meet their unique needs. Whether a customer is typing in a query, browsing through product categories on a website, or interacting via voice or image, our service springs into action. We analyze billions of search queries and tens of millions of products to identify the most relevant, high-value options for our customers. As a key member of our team, you will take end-to-end ownership of building cutting-edge systems that drive product discovery. This includes analyzing massive datasets, designing complex machine learning models, improving their accuracy and performance, and deploying these solutions to deliver an optimized shopping experience for millions of customers. You'll have the opportunity to tackle fascinating and high-impact problems in machine learning, information retrieval, natural language processing (NLP), and computer vision. Your work will directly influence how customers navigate the website, find the best products, and make purchase decisions with confidence. About Team: Our team works closely with our US stores and eCommerce business to better serve customers by empowering team members, stores, and merchants with technological innovation. From groceries and entertainment to sporting goods and crafts, Walmart U.S. offers an extensive selection that our customers value, whether they shop online at Walmart.com, through one of our mobile apps, or in-store. Focus areas include customers, stores and employees, in-store service, merchant tools, merchant data science, and search and personalization. What you'll do: · Applying machine learning ranking algorithms to maximize the relevance of search system and minimize risks. · Building, validating, testing and deploying predictive models using machine learning techniques to explain or predict behavior and solve a variety of business and engineering problems. · Identifying, collecting, and exploring the right data used for predictive modeling and algorithm development. · Performing and leading developments using machine learning techniques in one or more of the key areas like natural language processing, computer vision, information retrieval, entity recognition, product classification, and recommender system. What you'll bring: · You have 7+ years of work and research experience in the machine learning field with a deep understanding of machine learning and interest in applying it at scale. · You have experience with data cleaning, preparation, and featurization and selection techniques. · You have experience working with large data sets to solve problems. · You have experience with deep learning library like TensorFlow and/or PyTorch is highly desirable as well as experience with Spark and/or Hadoop. · You have/had hands-on experience with Python, Java or Scala and the ability to write reusable and efficient code to automate machine learning pipeline and data processes. · You have effective communication, interpersonal and teamwork skills. · You have the ability to handle multiple concurrent projects while working independently and in teams. · You can work in a fast-paced and deadline driven environment. · Ph.D. or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, Operations Research, Mathematics, Electrical Engineering, Computational Linguistics or related field. About Walmart Global Tech Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered. We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to

results.title: Walmart tries to persuade staff AI will 'improve jobs, not replace them' - Retail Gazette
highlights:
Walmart has moved to reassure its 2.1m employees that artificial intelligence will improve their roles, rather than threaten them, as it accelerates its use of AI across the business.
[...]
The US giant, which is the country’s largest private sector employer, used its annual Associates Week gathering in Arkansas to set out how AI is being used across areas including product design, logistics, store operations and customer service.
[...]
The company also announced that any US member of staff can now become certified in the use of OpenAI tools.
[...]
The push comes amid growing concern over the impact AI could have on jobs, particularly entry-level and administrative roles. According to job placement firm Challenger, Gray and Christmas, AI has been the leading reason US companies have cited for job cuts over the past three months.
[...]
Walmart shareholders also used the retailer’s annual general meeting on Thursday to call for a report into how AI could affect workers, although the proposal was voted down.
[...]
Walmart stepped up its AI investment last year with the appointment of Daniel Danker, formerly of grocery technology firm Instacart, as executive vice-president for AI acceleration, product and design.
[...]
spikes in demand
[...]
also highlighted examples of AI being used to reduce empty miles for freight drivers,
[...]
customer feedback for product development and support self-checkout systems that can identify loose produce without bar codes.
[...]
Incoming chief executive John Furner also praised two Walmart engineers who built a “vibe coding” platform now being used across the company, enabling hourly-paid employees to create code to solve business problems.
[...]
Walmart has moved to reassure its 2.1m employees that artificial intelligence will improve their roles, rather than threaten them, as it accelerates its use of AI across the business.
[...]
The US giant, which is the country’s largest private sector employer, used its annual Associates Week gathering in Arkansas to set out how AI is being used across areas including product design, logistics, store operations and customer service.
[...]
The company also announced that any US member of staff can now become certified in the use of OpenAI tools.
[...]
The push comes amid growing concern over the impact AI could have on jobs, particularly entry-level and administrative roles. According to job placement firm Challenger, Gray and Christmas, AI has been the leading reason US companies have cited for job cuts over the past three months.

[...]

[...]
Walmart stepped up its AI investment last year with the appointment of Daniel Danker, formerly of grocery technology firm Instacart, as executive vice-president for AI acceleration, product and design.
[...]
Danker said AI could help the retailer move from a fixed approach to stocking and shipping products towards a more predictive model, allowing Walmart to better anticipate spikes in demand.
[...]
Managers also highlighted examples of AI being used to reduce empty miles for freight drivers, summarise customer feedback for product development and support self-checkout systems that can identify loose produce without bar codes.
[...]
Incoming chief executive John Furner also praised two Walmart engineers who built a “vibe coding” platform now being used across the company, enabling hourly-paid employees to create code to solve business problems.
[...]
United for Respect, which sponsored the shareholder proposal, argued that the company should publish more detail on AI’s impact on workers. Walmart employee Ava Williams, speaking on behalf of the group, said a rushed rollout of new tools risked increasing pressure on staff.
